# Collision Shapes

> Primitive collision shapes for physics and raycasting

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** integration · godot

## What this does

Primitive collision shapes for physics and raycasting

Specifies 6 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## What must be true

- **implementation:** Feature implemented in C++ engine core
- **platform_agnostic:** Works across desktop, web, mobile platforms

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Box Shape** — when Axis-aligned bounding box is requested, then Axis-aligned bounding box completed.
- **Sphere Shape** — when Spherical collision is requested, then Spherical collision completed.
- **Capsule Shape** — when Cylinder with rounded ends is requested, then Cylinder with rounded ends completed.
- **Cylinder Shape** — when Circular extrusion is requested, then Circular extrusion completed.
- **Mesh Shape** — when Trimesh from model data is requested, then Trimesh from model data completed.
- **Convex Shape** — when Optimized convex hull is requested, then Optimized convex hull completed.
